title: Option of rights at Pôle Emploi
description: What is the right of option in Pôle Emploi and under which conditions can I exercise it to obtain a higher daily allowance or recharge my benefits?
narrative: The user is seeking a detailed explanation of the "right of option" (droit d’option) offered by Pôle Emploi. They want to know exactly when the right is available – such as after a period of unemployment, when rebooting their old rights before they expire, or when re‑enrolling – and the criteria that must be met: the quantity of hours or days worked since the last entitlement opened, the specific thresholds (e.g., 910 hours or 130 days for the modern regime, or 150 hours for the earlier regime), the requirement that the new daily allowance be at least 30 % higher than the remaining allowance or not exceed €20 per day, and the time limits (e.g., 3 years plus the original entitlement period). They also need to understand the procedural steps (applying to a counsellor, 21‑day decision window), the possible outcomes (recharging of old rights, receiving the new allowance based on last wages, or potential denial and eligibility for the Special Solidarity Allowance). The relevance criteria focus on documents that clarify these conditions, thresholds, deadlines, procedural requirements, and implications of using or not using the right of option.”}
